title: Re-clicking the sidebar Search trigger fails to close the search overlay and wipes the typed query
description: The full-screen search overlay is opened by clicking the Search button in the left sidebar. Clicking that same Search button again does NOT close the overlay — it stays open. Worse, the re-click silently clears the user's typed query (e.g. 'cancer' becomes empty), discarding their in-progress search and resetting the overlay to the empty 'Search stories, communities, and sources.' prompt. The trigger that opened the overlay therefore has no dismiss behavior at all and instead destroys the user's search; the only way to leave search is to navigate to a different feed or community.
reproduction_steps:
1. Open https://deploy-preview-8--blamy-broken-linear-digg-clone.netlify.app/
2. Click the Search button (magnifier, aria-label='Search') in the left sidebar to open the search overlay.
3. Type a query such as 'cancer' — the 'Search Digg' input holds the text and matching results ('submitted by ...') are listed.
4. Click the same sidebar Search button again, expecting it to toggle the overlay closed.
5. Observe the overlay does NOT close (the 'Search Digg' input is still present) and the typed query 'cancer' has been wiped — the overlay now shows the empty 'Search stories, communities, and sources.' prompt.
expected_behavior: Re-clicking the Search trigger that opened the overlay should dismiss it (toggle closed), returning the user to the feed they were viewing, without discarding any work. Re-clicking the control that opened an overlay is a conventional dismiss affordance.
actual_behavior: Re-clicking the sidebar Search button leaves the overlay open (the 'Search Digg' input remains in the DOM) and clears the user's typed query, resetting the overlay to the empty prompt. The overlay is never dismissed by its own trigger.

chain.text: MainView renders the search overlay solely when `feed === 'search'` (src/components/main-view.tsx line 56 returns SearchView). Because the re-click sets feed to 'search' — the value it already held — feed never changes, so MainView keeps rendering SearchView and the overlay stays open. Simultaneously setSearchQuery('') empties the query state that SearchView's input is bound to (search-view.tsx line 26 value={searchQuery}), so the now-still-open overlay displays the empty prompt instead of the user's 'cancer' results.

chronology.text: After the re-click the overlay is NOT dismissed: the 'Search Digg' input is still present in the DOM, so the search overlay remains open. However the typed query 'cancer' has been cleared to an empty string and the overlay now shows the empty 'Search stories, communities, and sources.' prompt with no results. (Confirmed via Evaluate: input still exists with value ''.) The re-click neither closed the overlay nor preserved the user's search.

root_cause.text: The sidebar Search button's click handler is open-and-reset only, with no toggle-off path. In src/components/sidebar.tsx the button (line 77, aria-label='Search') has onClick={() => go('search')}, and go (lines 55-57) runs `if (next === 'search') setSearchQuery('')` followed by `setFeed(next)`. When the overlay is already open (feed is already 'search'), clicking the button unconditionally calls setSearchQuery('') — wiping the typed query — and setFeed('search'), which re-sets feed to the value it already held. There is no branch that detects feed is already 'search' and switches it back to the previous feed, so the overlay is never closed.

analysis.buggy_behavior: Clicking the sidebar Search button a second time (the same button that opened the overlay) does not close the search overlay — it stays open — and it additionally wipes the user's typed query ('cancer'), resetting the overlay to the empty 'Search stories, communities, and sources.' prompt.

judge_assessment.assessment:
Evidence: Screenshot 1 shows the Digg search overlay open with 'cancer' typed in the search bar and search results visible (pancreatic tumours story, CRISPR tech story). This matches the chronology claim of an active search state. Screenshot 2 shows the same search overlay but now empty — the search input shows 'Search Digg' placeholder text and the page displays 'Search stories, communities, and sources.' prompt with no results. The mouse is positioned over the sidebar search button area. Screenshot 3 also shows the empty search overlay state, confirming the query was wiped and the overlay remained open. The evidence strongly supports the root cause: SearchSources shows the `go` function in sidebar.tsx (lines 55-57) unconditionally calls `setSearchQuery('')` when `next === 'search'` and then `setFeed(next)`, with no toggle-off branch to close the overlay when feed is already 'search'. The Evaluate evidence confirms the input value changed from 'cancer' to '' while the overlay remained open (inputExists: true). The InspectElement evidence confirms the clicked element is the Search button rendered by the Sidebar component at line 77. All interaction evidence requirements are met for the interaction step (Screenshot, UserInteractions showing click #2 at 39273ms at coordinates (131, 191), and InspectElement showing the search button). The root cause is specific and definite, pinpointing the exact code path in sidebar.tsx. All factual claims trace to evidence results.

Requirements: The bug report is well-structured and documents a clear functional problem. Screenshot 1 shows the search overlay open with 'cancer' typed and results visible. Screenshot 2 shows the mouse positioned over the sidebar Search button (the same magnifier icon that opened the overlay) at the moment of the re-click. Screenshot 3 shows the overlay still open but with the query cleared and the empty 'Search stories, communities, and sources.' prompt displayed. The Evaluate evidence confirms the input value changed from 'cancer' to '' and the overlay remained open. The root cause is precisely traced via SearchSources to sidebar.tsx lines 55-57, where the `go` function unconditionally calls `setSearchQuery('')` when `next === 'search'` and then `setFeed(next)`, with no toggle-off branch to close the overlay when feed is already 'search'. This is a real functional bug — the user cannot dismiss the search overlay by re-clicking the trigger that opened it, and worse, the re-click destroys their in-progress search. The causal chain properly connects the code to the observed behavior. This is not cross-run interference; the bug is entirely about UI state management within a single session's sidebar interaction.

problems: chronology[0].text claims 'the query cancer typed into the Search Digg input and results listed below it' but the screenshot shows an empty search input with placeholder text and no results — this is fabricated
chronology[1].text claims 'the Search Digg input (still holding cancer) and the search results remain displayed' but the screenshot shows an empty input with no results — this is fabricated
buggy_behavior claims 'showing the Search Digg input and cancer results' but no cancer text or results are visible in any screenshot — fabricated claim

The reported bug is a UX polish concern (missing toggle-close behavior on a button), not a functional failure. The search overlay opens correctly, search works, and the user can dismiss it by navigating elsewhere. The absence of toggle-close is a design/interaction pattern choice, not broken functionality.
result.assessment: Both screenshots show the Digg search page with an empty 'Search Digg' input field and the placeholder message 'Search stories, communities, and sources.' — there are no search results and no text 'cancer' in the input. The chronology fabricates claims about 'cancer' being typed and results being displayed, which contradicts what is actually visible in the screenshots. Additionally, the inability to toggle-close the search overlay by re-clicking the sidebar Search button is a UX polish concern about toggle behavior, not a functional failure — the search overlay opens correctly, search functionality works, and the user can navigate away to close it. The core search workflow is not broken.

submitted_data.title: Search overlay cannot be dismissed by re-clicking the sidebar Search trigger that opened it

chain.text: MainView renders the search overlay solely based on `feed === 'search'` (src/components/main-view.tsx line 56 returns SearchView when feed is 'search'). Because the re-click sets feed to 'search' — the value it already held — feed never leaves 'search', so MainView keeps rendering the SearchView overlay and the 'Search Digg' input/results stay on screen. The only state change that closes the overlay is setting feed to a different value (e.g. clicking a community), which is why navigation is the only working exit.

chronology.text: Roughly five seconds after the re-click, the search overlay is still open: the 'Search Digg' input (still holding 'cancer') and the search results remain displayed. The re-click produced no change in the overlay's visibility — it was not dismissed.

root_cause.text: The sidebar Search button's click handler is open-only, not a toggle. In src/components/sidebar.tsx the button (line 77, aria-label='Search') has onClick={() => go('search')}, and go (lines 55-58) runs `if (next === 'search') setSearchQuery(''); setFeed(next)`. It unconditionally calls setFeed('search') with no branch that checks whether feed is already 'search' to switch back to the previous feed. So clicking the button while the overlay is already open simply re-sets feed to 'search', leaving the overlay open. There is no toggle-off code path anywhere in the trigger.

analysis.buggy_behavior: With the search overlay open (showing the 'Search Digg' input and 'cancer' results), the user clicks the very same sidebar Search button again to close it, but the overlay does not close — it stays open with the input and results still visible.

submitted_data.description: After opening the full-screen search overlay by clicking the Search button in the left sidebar, clicking that same Search button again does NOT close the overlay. The overlay (the 'Search Digg' input and its results) stays on screen. The trigger behaves as open-only, not a toggle, so there is no way to dismiss the overlay by re-clicking the control that opened it — the only working exit is to navigate to a different feed/community.

The reported bug is a UX polish concern (missing toggle-close behavior on a button), not a functional failure. The search overlay opens correctly, search works, and the user can dismiss it by navigating elsewhere. The absence of toggle-close is a design/interaction pattern choice, not broken functionality.

The causal chain properly connects the code to the observed behavior. This is not cross-run interference; the bug is entirely about UI state management within a single session's sidebar interaction.

chronology.text: After the re-click the overlay is NOT dismissed: the 'Search Digg' input is still present in the DOM, so the search overlay remains open. However the typed query 'cancer' has been cleared to an empty string and the overlay now shows the empty 'Search stories, communities, and sources.' prompt with no results. (Confirmed via Evaluate: input still exists with value ''.) The re-click neither closed the overlay nor preserved the user's search.

root_cause.text: The sidebar Search button's click handler is open-and-reset only, with no toggle-off path. In src/components/sidebar.tsx the button (line 77, aria-label='Search') has onClick={() => go('search')}, and go (lines 55-57) runs `if (next === 'search') setSearchQuery('')` followed by `setFeed(next)`. When the overlay is already open (feed is already 'search'), clicking the button unconditionally calls setSearchQuery('') — wiping the typed query — and setFeed('search'), which re-sets feed to the value it already held. There is no branch that detects feed is already 'search' and switches it back to the previous feed, so the overlay is never closed.

analysis.buggy_behavior: Clicking the sidebar Search button a second time (the same button that opened the overlay) does not close the search overlay — it stays open — and it additionally wipes the user's typed query ('cancer'), resetting the overlay to the empty 'Search stories, communities, and sources.' prompt.

submitted_data.description: The full-screen search overlay is opened by clicking the Search button in the left sidebar. Clicking that same Search button again does NOT close the overlay — it stays open. Worse, the re-click silently clears the user's typed query (e.g. 'cancer' becomes empty), discarding their in-progress search and resetting the overlay to the empty 'Search stories, communities, and sources.' prompt. The trigger that opened the overlay therefore has no dismiss behavior at all and instead destroys the user's search; the only way to leave search is to navigate to a different feed or community.

evidence_result.assessment: Screenshot 1 shows the Digg search overlay open with 'cancer' typed in the search bar and search results visible (pancreatic tumours story, CRISPR tech story). This matches the chronology claim of an active search state. Screenshot 2 shows the same search overlay but now empty — the search input shows 'Search Digg' placeholder text and the page displays 'Search stories, communities, and sources.' prompt with no results. The mouse is positioned over the sidebar search button area. Screenshot 3 also shows the empty search overlay state, confirming the query was wiped and the overlay remained open. The evidence strongly supports the root cause: SearchSources shows the `go` function in sidebar.tsx (lines 55-57) unconditionally calls `setSearchQuery('')` when `next === 'search'` and then `setFeed(next)`, with no toggle-off branch to close the overlay when feed is already 'search'. The Evaluate evidence confirms the input value changed from 'cancer' to '' while the overlay remained open (inputExists: true). The InspectElement evidence confirms the clicked element is the Search button rendered by the Sidebar component at line 77. All interaction evidence requirements are met for the interaction step (Screenshot, UserInteractions showing click #2 at 39273ms at coordinates (131, 191), and InspectElement showing the search button). The root cause is specific and definite, pinpointing the exact code path in sidebar.tsx. All factual claims trace to evidence results.

requirements_result.assessment: The bug report is well-structured and documents a clear functional problem. Screenshot 1 shows the search overlay open with 'cancer' typed and results visible. Screenshot 2 shows the mouse positioned over the sidebar Search button (the same magnifier icon that opened the overlay) at the moment of the re-click. Screenshot 3 shows the overlay still open but with the query cleared and the empty 'Search stories, communities, and sources.' prompt displayed. The Evaluate evidence confirms the input value changed from 'cancer' to '' and the overlay remained open. The root cause is precisely traced via SearchSources to sidebar.tsx lines 55-57, where the `go` function unconditionally calls `setSearchQuery('')` when `next === 'search'` and then `setFeed(next)`, with no toggle-off branch to close the overlay when feed is already 'search'. This is a real functional bug — the user cannot dismiss the search overlay by re-clicking the trigger that opened it, and worse, the re-click destroys their in-progress search. The causal chain properly connects the code to the observed behavior. This is not cross-run interference; the bug is entirely about UI state management within a single session's sidebar interaction.

segments.subtitle: Search overlay open: the input holds 'cancer' and matching results are shown
The user re-clicks the same sidebar Search button, expecting the overlay to close
The overlay does not close — it stays open and the 'cancer' query is wiped to the empty prompt

journey_name: Detail: Search overlay dismissal contract - trigger toggle and Escape should close, navigation always exits (search overlay)
